text.title: Recognition Rather Than Recall Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Recognition Rather Than Recall. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Multi-step workflows show prior selections persistently on later steps.
rule.rationale: Multi-step workflows show prior selections persistently on later steps.

rule.statement: Frequent option sets show a 'Recently used' surface populated from user history.
rule.rationale: Frequent option sets show a 'Recently used' surface populated from user history.

rule.statement: Large option sets have autosuggest with ≤3-char prefix matching.
rule.rationale: Large option sets have autosuggest with ≤3-char prefix matching.

rule.statement: Reference data (limits, codes) is one click away, not memorised.
rule.rationale: Reference data (limits, codes) is one click away, not memorised.

rule.statement: Forms have defaults derived from context, not blank fields requiring recall.
rule.rationale: Forms have defaults derived from context, not blank fields requiring recall.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-recognition-over-recall.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
